name: lotus seat SEO/AEO architecture
description: Applied the Island Internships playbook to thelotusseat.com: hub-and-spoke keyword pages with zero Q&A overlap. Hub /meditation-cushion-guide (zafu vs bench vs seat comparison table), spokes /meditation-cushion-for-back-pain and /vipassana-meditation-cushion, with / holding the head term 'meditation seat'. Every page: visible FAQ accordion + FAQPage JSON-LD reading from the SAME data module (src/app/faq/faqs.ts, src/components/home/homeFaqs.ts) so schema text can never drift from rendered text. Schema builders in src/lib/seo/schema.ts use a cross-referenced @graph with @id so crawlers resolve one entity, not four. Deliberately NO aggregateRating/review — product is pre-order and fake reviews are a manual-action risk.

name: AEO: extractable answer blocks + llms.txt
description: AI-search optimization pattern that is distinct from classic SEO: (1) public/llms.txt with an entity-defining blockquote, spec bullets, an honest-limits section, and a page index; (2) an AnswerBlock component carrying data-answer, holding a 60-90 word self-contained passage right under each H1 that survives being lifted out of the page with no context; (3) Article schema speakable cssSelector pointing at [data-answer]; (4) hero copy leading with subject-predicate ('The Lotus Seat is an ergonomic meditation seat...') because answer engines quote definitions, not adjectives; (5) robots.ts explicitly ALLOWS GPTBot/ClaudeBot/PerplexityBot — for a pre-order product with no backlinks, answer engines are the only channel where it can compete.

An external AI conversion teardown of thelotusseat.com ranked as its #1 A/B test: hero CTA copy 'Pre-order — no charge until ship'. This is FALSE for this codebase — src/app/api/checkout/route.ts calls createOrder with the full totalMinorUnits and the webhook listens for payment.captured, so the card is charged on submit. src/components/home/assurances.ts documents that this exact claim was previously on the homepage and was deliberately REMOVED as untrue. The same teardown also recommended a fabricated proof strip ('2,000+ sits tested'), inventing a testimonial from non-existent beta users, a false scarcity counter, and an unqualified health claim ('without pain') that contradicts PRODUCT.md's anti-wellness positioning. It also 're-recommended' several features that already shipped (secondary CTA, spec ticker, comparison table, FAQ accordion) — its per-bullet [1] citations to the homepage were decorative.
resolution: Audit any external/AI marketing teardown against the actual payment code and PRODUCT.md before implementing. For a pre-order that charges on submit, de-risk with the CANCELLATION RIGHT ('cancel any time before dispatch, full refund'), never with a payment-timing claim. Never ship fabricated social proof on a pre-order product — it is also why Product JSON-LD carries no aggregateRating/review.
